headline: MPs to debate conditions on UK arms exports amid civilian-harm reports
blurb: A backbench debate is scheduled in the Commons on whether to attach human-rights conditions to UK arms export licences, following reports of civilian harm. Campaigners say licensing has continued despite the reports; the government says each licence is assessed against existing criteria. No binding vote is expected, but the debate shapes the pressure ministers feel. This is a placeholder item written in-house to demonstrate the format. Replace it with real curation.
callPoints: Ask where your MP stands on attaching conditions to export licences
Raise the civilian-harm reports campaigners have cited
Ask whether your MP will speak or intervene in the debate
emailContext: The Commons is due to debate whether to attach human-rights conditions to UK arms export licences after reports of civilian harm.

headline: Council debates funding for reporting after rise in incidents at local mosques
blurb: Following a reported rise in vandalism and threats at local mosques, a council is weighing a budget line for a community-safety reporting scheme. Supporters say it would speed response and improve data; some residents have questions about how the data would be used. A decision is expected next month. Placeholder copy. Replace with a real, sourced story and your own blurb.
callPoints: Ask your MP's view on supporting community-safety funding locally
Raise support for, or questions about, how incident data would be handled
emailContext: A council is considering funding a community-safety reporting scheme after a rise in reported incidents at local mosques; a decision is expected next month.
